title: GameStop Q1 2025 Earnings Report Summary
text: GameStop reported mixed earnings for Q1 2025, achieving an earnings per share of $0.17, surpassing analyst expectations of $0.08. However, revenue came in at $732.4 million, falling short of the anticipated $750 million target. Following the earnings announcement, GameStop's stock experienced a significant decline. Shares dropped to as low as $28.52 in after-hours trading, compared to the closing price of $30.24 earlier that day. The report revealed a bright spot in GameStop's collectibles division, which demonstrated robust growth with collectibles revenue growing by 54 percent in the quarter, marking a substantial increase in this business segment. Despite beating profit expectations, the company's failure to meet revenue forecasts prompted investor concern and triggered a notable selloff in GME stock during extended trading hours. The earnings results showcased GameStop's ongoing struggle to boost top-line revenue while simultaneously improving operational efficiency and profitability through cost control measures.
